title: thornbera grayi
intro_study: According to "Journal of complementary & integrative medicine", thornbera grayi, which is also known as Phragmenthera capitata, may be beneficial in fighting against melanoma due to its high antioxidant activity, high phenolic content, and its ability to promote apoptosis-induced cell death in cancer cells.
side_effects: Thornbera grayi can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, headaches, and allergic reactions. Ingesting the plant's leaves or stems can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils can lead to skin irritation, rashes, and inflammation in sensitive individuals. In severe cases, improper use of Thornbera grayi can also trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
